title: Claude Design Basics | Master 95% in 10 Minutes
summary: Tristen O'Brien presents a 6-step framework for using Claude Design, Anthropic's new design tool. The video demonstrates building landing pages, pitch decks, and mobile app prototypes from text prompts without coding or design experience, covering interface basics through export and handoff to Claude Code.
writeup: Claude Design (claude.ai/design) is Anthropic's experimental web-only tool for generating high-fidelity UI prototypes, animated slide decks, and interactive app mockups from natural language prompts. The video traces a six-step framework from interface orientation through design systems, establishing that the design system feature is the differentiating layer that locks generated output to a specific brand rather than Claude's arbitrary style choices. The through-line is that Claude Design is a rapid prototyping surface, and the natural handoff path for functional applications with databases and user accounts is Claude Code.
key_points: Design systems are the central feature of Claude Design: once a brand blueprint (specific colors, fonts, button styles, spacing) is created and stored, every subsequent artifact, whether a website, pitch deck, or app, carries that identity instead of Claude's own default style choices.

Claude Design generates three artifact types from the same workspace: high-fidelity interactive prototypes, animated presentation slide decks, and mobile app prototypes with working navigation across multiple screens; before building anything, it asks clarifying questions about audience, visual style, layout preferences, and section count.

Four refinement methods are available on the canvas: direct text editing by clicking any element, element-specific comment instructions that Claude acts on in isolation, freehand drawing that Claude interprets and converts into built UI components, and a tweaks palette for swapping color schemes, dark/light mode, and layout variations without re-prompting.

The most powerful export option is handing a finished design directly to Claude Code, which is the required path for adding a database, user accounts, or payment flows that Claude Design itself cannot produce.

Claude Design has its own separate weekly token limit that depletes quickly during complex projects; the recommended approach is to start with a simple slide deck or single-page prototype to learn the tool's behavior before committing to larger builds.

The tool is a research preview on the web only (not in the Claude desktop or mobile apps), requires at minimum the $20/month Pro plan, and carries known bugs including text overlap in generated outputs.
